person.name: Elizabeth Hollis
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Johanna Hollis was born about 1566, probably at Saffron Walden, Essex, England. Johanna married, on 20 Jan 1593 in Saffron Walden, John Mott. John was born about 1571 in Saffron Walden, and died 1656 in Portsmouth, Newport, Rhode Island. Johanna and John had at least one child, Adam Mott, born about 1596. Johanna (Hollis) Mott passed away at Saffron Walden around or after her son's date of birth.

John Mott married:

Johanna Hollis, married on 20 Jan 1593 in Saffron Walden, England
Elizabeth, born about 1575 in Saffron Walden, Essex, England

«b»Children:«/b»
1.) Adam Mott, born about 1596 in Saffron Walden, Essex, England

«b»The following is from The American Genealogist Vol. 20, page 227-228:«/b»

MOTT, Adam (p344, 1st col.) was the son of "oulde John Mott" who was admitted a freeman at Aquidneck in 1638. In 8 mo.: 1639 John Mott's land is mentiond. On 29 Aug. 1644, 25 Jan. 1648/9, 2 June 1650, 19 Jan 1651/2, 9 June 1652, 2 June 1653, 20 June 1653 and 17 June 1654, the town of Portsmouth, R. I. provided for his care (Records of the Town of Portsmouth). On 23 Jan 1654/5, the town agreed to pay the passage of "ould John Mott to Barbados Iland and back again if he cannot be received there, if he live to it, if the shipp owners will carried him." (ib., p. 66) On 3 July 1656 further provision was made for his keep and "the oulde mans son Adam" engaged to give a cow and a supply of corn towards it (ib. p. 72). The ould man was evidently held in high esteem, for the order of 9 June 1652 provided "that there shalbe a stone house built for the more comfortabl beinge of ould John Mott in the winter" (ib. p. 58).

«b»Notes«/b»
John Mott was admitted as an inhabitant of Aquidneck Island in 1638, probably with his son Adam Mott. It is not known when or on what ship he came to the colonies.

He was called Oulde John Mott and received care from the town of Portsmouth for several years from 29 Aug 1644 to his death, probably about 1656.

The following entries from Saffron Walden may pertain to Adam Mott's father (or his grandfather) John Mott:
Buried 2 Jan. 1610/11, Elizabeth wife of John Mott;
Buried 4 May 1619, Catherine wife of John Mott;
Buried 2 Jan 1616/17, Ann daughter of John Mott; and
Baptized 29 Sept. 1625, Elizabeth daughter of John and Mary Mott.

sourceList.title: Essex, England, Church of England Baptisms, Marriages and Burials, 1538-1812
Mot in entry for Sarah Mot, "England, Essex Parish Registers, 1538-1997"
London Marriage Licenses 1521-1869 By Joseph Foster, John Ward Dean, Joseph Lemuel Chester · 1887

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Hollis於大約1566年出生在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登時，她的父親William Hollis27歲，母親Sarah Lees22歲。她於1593年1月20日與John Mott II在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她於1901年住在英國英格蘭德比夏爾哈斯恩。她去世於1610年1月2日之前在英國英格蘭艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登，埋葬在英國艾可布拉德維爾--希亞。

nameMeanings.name: Hollis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands): topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) holly or holly trees’, from Middle English holi(e), holin (Old English holegn, holen). Compare Hollen and Holley 3.

variants.name: Holley
meanings: nickname from Middle English hol ‘hollow’ + eie, egh(e) ‘eye’ (Old English hol + ēage), perhaps a nickname for someone with a missing eye.
topographic name for someone who lived by a ‘clearing in a hollow’, from Middle English hol ‘hollow’ + lei(e) ‘woodland clearing’.

variants.name: Hollins
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called Hollins (Staffordshire, Lancashire, Yorkshire), or else a topographic name from the plural of Middle English holin ‘holly’ (see Hollis ).

variants.name: Holm
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Anders, Thor, Holger, Nels, Nils, Sven, Helmer, Lennart, Niels, Vibeke.

English, Scottish, German, and Scandinavian: topographic name for someone who lived on an island, in particular a piece of slightly raised land lying in a fen or partly surrounded by streams, from Middle English, Middle Low German holm, Old Norse holmr, or else a habitational name from a place called with this element. The Swedish name is often ornamental.

English: topographic name for someone who lived where holly grew, from Middle English holm, a divergent development of Old English hole(g)n ‘holly (tree)’; or, especially in southern England, a habitational name from any of the places such as East and West Holme (Dorset) or Holne (Devon). Compare Hollis .

variants.name: Hollen
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a holly tree, from Middle English holin ‘holly’ (Old English holegn). Compare Hollis .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Hollis was born about 1566, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, her father, William Hollis, was 27 and her mother, Sarah Lees, was 22. She married John Mott II on 20 January 1593, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She lived in Hartshorne, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom in 1901. She died before 2 January 1610, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Bradwell-on-Sea, Essex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hollisが約1566年にイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンで生まれた時，父William Hollisは27歳，母Sarah Leesは22歳であった。1593年1月20日にイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンでJohn Mott IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1901年にはイギリスイギリスデルブイシュアイアハートショルンに住んでいた。彼女は1610年1月2日以前にイギリスイギリスエセックスサフロン ワルデンで死去し，イギリスエセックスブラドウエル-オン-セアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Hollis(이)가 대략 1566년 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Hollis(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sarah Lees(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1593년 1월 20일 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 John Mott II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1901년도에 영국 잉글랜드 더비실르 하츠호른에서 살았다. 그녀는 1610년 1월 2일 이전 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 사프런 월든에서 사망했으며 영국 에세스 브라드웰--시에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Hollis nasceu aproximadamente 1566, em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Hollis, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Sarah Lees, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com John Mott II em 20 de janeiro de 1593, em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela viveu em Hartshorne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom em 1901. Ela faleceu antes 2 de janeiro de 1610, em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Bradwell-on-Sea, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.St. Andrew, Holburn, London, England: ст андрю холборн абов т барс вит ст джеордж т мартир, холборн, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Hollis родилась около 1566 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени William Hollis было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah Lees, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Mott II 20 января 1593 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она жила в местности харцхаурн, дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания в 1901 году. Она умерла перед 2 январь 1610 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности брадвелл-он-сеа, эссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Hollis nació aproximadamente 1566, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, William Hollis, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Sarah Lees, tenía 22 años. Se casó con John Mott II el 20 de enero de 1593, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Vivió en Hartshorne, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido en 1901. Murió antes de 2 de enero de 1610, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Bradwell-on-Sea, Essex, Inglaterra.
